description: Hand-curated 50-query temporal-inference fixture modelled on LongMemEval's temporal-reasoning category. Tests date recall, relative-time resolution, event sequencing, knowledge-update ordering, and recency ranking across multi-session conversation haystacks.
notes: Path C (hand-curated representative subset). LongMemEval source: https://huggingface.co/datasets/xiaowu0162/longmemeval-cleaned (MIT license per HuggingFace dataset card). Data here is original; question/answer patterns are inspired by the published category taxonomy. Full LongMemEval temporal-inference fixture (all ~133 questions converted from the real dataset) lands in v0.1. To regenerate from the real dataset once licensed: see scripts/convert-longmemeval.ts.

items.content:
user: I signed up for a gym membership today at FitLife downtown.
assistant: Great! Which membership tier did you go with?
user: The annual plan — $480 for the year. They have a pool which was the deciding factor.
assistant: That's a solid deal. Have you checked out their class schedule yet?
user: Not yet, starting Monday.

user: Finished my first two weeks at the gym. Went four times total.
assistant: That's a solid start for January. Did you try the pool?
user: Twice. The lap lanes are busy mornings but empty at 7pm.
assistant: Good to know. Setting any specific goals?
user: Want to swim 1km without stopping by March.

user: I cancelled my gym membership today.
assistant: Oh — what happened?
user: I'm moving to a new apartment in March that has its own gym included. No point paying double.
assistant: Makes sense. Did FitLife give you a refund for the remaining months?
user: Pro-rated refund of $360 back to my card.

user: I got a job offer today from Meridian Tech — senior backend engineer role.
assistant: Congratulations! How are you feeling about it?
user: Excited but nervous. The salary is $145k which is a 20% bump from my current role.
assistant: That's a meaningful increase. When do they need a decision by?
user: January 17th.

user: I accepted the Meridian Tech offer.
assistant: Great news! When do you start?
user: February 19th. I gave two weeks notice at my current job today.
assistant: How did your current manager take it?
user: Professionally — she said she expected it since I'd been interviewing.

user: First day at Meridian Tech done. It was overwhelming in a good way.
assistant: What was the biggest surprise?
user: The codebase is much cleaner than I expected for a 7-year-old startup. Strong TypeScript discipline throughout.
assistant: Always a good sign. Did you meet the full team?
user: Most of them — 11 engineers total on the backend squad.

user: Just got back from the dentist. First time in two years, I was dreading it.
assistant: How did it go?
user: Better than expected. One small cavity on the upper right molar, otherwise clean.
assistant: Are you going back for the filling?
user: Scheduled for February 5th.

user: Got my cavity filled this morning.
assistant: All done — how was it?
user: Fine. Local anesthetic, took about 25 minutes. Mouth was numb for a few hours.
assistant: Glad it went smoothly. Any follow-up needed?
user: Just a six-month checkup in August.

user: Moving day! Finally in the new apartment.
assistant: How did the move go?
user: Long but smooth. Hired two movers, done by 2pm.
assistant: What neighborhood did you end up in?
user: Eastside — about 15 minutes from Meridian Tech's office, which is the main draw.
assistant: Shorter commute than before?
user: Way shorter. Was 45 minutes on transit, now 12 minutes on the bike path.

user: Two weeks into bike commuting. Loving it.
assistant: Any issues so far?
user: One flat tire on day four — patched it myself. Otherwise zero problems.
assistant: Are you tracking the distance?
user: About 24km a week round trip.

user: Started reading 'The Pragmatic Programmer' again — the 20th anniversary edition.
assistant: A classic re-read. What's prompting it?
user: Starting a new role next month, want to refresh my engineering fundamentals.
assistant: Good timing. Are you working through exercises or just reading?
user: Exercises too this time.

user: Finished 'The Pragmatic Programmer'. Took me 23 days.
assistant: Solid pace for a technical book with exercises. Top takeaway?
user: The section on orthogonality — applies directly to the microservices mess at my current job.
assistant: Good timing before the switch.
user: Exactly.

user: Started 'Designing Data-Intensive Applications' by Kleppmann.
assistant: Great choice — the chapter on replication alone is worth the price.
user: I'm already on chapter 3. Reading faster because I'm more engaged.
assistant: What's drawing you to it?
user: Distributed systems will be a big part of the Meridian Tech role.

user: Done with DDIA. Took about seven weeks.
assistant: That's a thorough read for a dense book. Changed how you think about anything at work?
user: Already refactored how we approach our Kafka consumer group assignments based on the partition chapter.
assistant: That's the best kind of technical reading.
user: Agreed.

user: Started a side project this weekend — a CLI tool for managing dotfiles.
assistant: Nice. What's the stack?
user: Rust, using the clap crate for CLI parsing. First real Rust project.
assistant: Ambitious for a first Rust project. What's the core feature set?
user: Symlink management, template substitution, and machine profiles.

user: Paused the dotfiles CLI project for now.
assistant: What happened?
user: New job onboarding is eating all my energy. Good problem to have.
assistant: Makes sense to give the job priority in the first 90 days.
user: I'll pick it up again once I find a rhythm at Meridian.

user: Picked up the dotfiles CLI project again. Three months since I last touched it.
assistant: Welcome back. Did you manage to context-switch back in quickly?
user: Took a few hours but yes. Rust's ownership errors reminded me where I left off.
assistant: What's left to ship?
user: Template substitution — the last of the three core features.

user: Shipped v0.1 of the dotfiles CLI to GitHub today.
assistant: Milestone! How does it feel?
user: Great — took five months total but it works. Symlinks, templates, and machine profiles all functional.
assistant: Will you maintain it?
user: Yes. A coworker at Meridian already wants to try it.

user: Registered for QCon London in March. Three-day conference.
assistant: Nice. Any sessions you're particularly targeting?
user: The distributed systems track and a workshop on observability with OpenTelemetry.
assistant: Useful given the Meridian role.
user: That's exactly why I chose those.

user: Back from QCon London. Excellent conference.
assistant: What was the best session?
user: A talk on CRDT-based collaboration by a Figma engineer. Mind-expanding.
assistant: And the OpenTelemetry workshop?
user: Practical and immediately applicable — already planning to bring it to Meridian's on-call runbooks.

user: Started a Spanish course on Duolingo. Aiming for 20 minutes a day.
assistant: Great habit to build. Any particular reason for Spanish?
user: Planning a trip to Colombia in the autumn. Want to be conversational, not just tourist-level.
assistant: Practical motivation helps with consistency.
user: That's the plan.

user: Hit a 85-day Duolingo streak today.
assistant: That's impressive consistency — almost three months of daily practice.
user: It became part of my morning routine. Do it before coffee now.
assistant: How's the Spanish feeling?
user: I can follow slow conversations. Reading is better than listening.

user: Signed up for in-person Spanish classes. One session per week at the community center.
assistant: Upgrading from Duolingo — what triggered the shift?
user: I realized I needed conversation practice, not just vocabulary drilling. Duolingo doesn't do that well.
assistant: Smart move ahead of the Colombia trip.
user: That's the plan — classes until September then the trip.

user: Moved my index fund allocation today — shifted from 80/20 equities/bonds to 90/10.
assistant: More aggressive — what drove the change?
user: I'm 31, new higher-paying job. Time horizon is long and I can handle more volatility.
assistant: Sounds reasoned. What platform?
user: Fidelity — their FZROX and FXNAX funds.

user: Did a mid-year investment review. Up 9.3% YTD on the main portfolio.
assistant: Solid performance for the first half. Any rebalancing needed?
user: Still within my target bands — no action needed.
assistant: Sticking with the 90/10 split?
user: Yes. No reason to change it.

user: I adopted a dog today. 3-year-old greyhound mix named Theo.
assistant: Wonderful news! What's Theo like?
user: Calm, a bit shy at first, but already following me room to room. Very gentle.
assistant: Greyhounds are often couch potatoes despite the breed reputation.
user: The shelter said exactly that. Two walks a day should be enough.

user: Theo has been with me a week. He's already sleeping on the bed.
assistant: Fast work from Theo. Any settling-in challenges?
user: He was anxious the first two nights — pacing. Now he's completely relaxed.
assistant: Classic greyhound adjustment arc.
user: Vet appointment booked for May 3rd for a full checkup.

user: Theo's vet checkup came back clean. Healthy weight, good teeth.
assistant: Great news. Any vaccinations needed?
user: Bordetella booster due in six months, everything else current from the shelter.
assistant: Sounds like the shelter did good prep work.
user: Very professional organization — glad I went through them.

user: I asked for a raise today at Meridian — requested $162k from $145k.
assistant: Bold move at six weeks in. What was the reception?
user: My manager said she'd take it to the quarterly review in June.
assistant: Reasonable — you came in with the logic?
user: Yes: two production incidents resolved in my first month, plus the Kafka refactor.

user: Got my raise — $155k, up from $145k. Not the full ask but still 6.9%.
assistant: A meaningful outcome, especially this early. Were you expecting the full $162k?
user: Hoped for it. Happy with $155k — shows they value the contributions.
assistant: And sets a baseline for the next conversation.
user: Exactly — already thinking about the year-end review.

user: Switched to a pour-over setup at home. Got a Hario V60 and a hand grinder.
assistant: Nice upgrade. What prompted it?
user: My partner got me a bag of single-origin Ethiopian beans for my birthday and drip coffee didn't do them justice.
assistant: Smart pivot. How does the V60 treat you?
user: Takes 10 minutes but the cup quality is genuinely different.

user: Upgraded to an electric burr grinder — Baratza Encore.
assistant: Moving up in the pour-over world. What pushed you past the hand grinder?
user: Wrist fatigue on mornings before bike commutes. Wanted faster grinding.
assistant: The Encore is a solid entry into electric. Noticing a cup difference?
user: Slightly more consistent grind. The hand grinder was already good though.

user: I've been listening to 'The Changelog' podcast during my commute. Great for keeping up with the OSS ecosystem.
assistant: Good choice for staying current. Any episodes you've particularly valued recently?
user: One on the Deno vs Node.js state of play — clarified a lot for me.
assistant: Useful framing?
user: Yes, especially the module compatibility angle.

user: Switched my main commute podcast from 'The Changelog' to 'Software Engineering Daily'. More depth per episode.
assistant: A natural progression — Changelog is great for breadth, SE Daily for depth. What shifted the preference?
user: The bike commute is shorter now so I batch 2-3 episodes on the weekend instead.
assistant: Makes sense to shift to higher-signal-per-hour content.
user: Exactly.

user: My partner accepted a job offer at a design agency in the city. She starts March 4th.
assistant: Congratulations to her! Was it a long process?
user: Three rounds of interviews over six weeks. Her portfolio work for the previous agency really landed.
assistant: A big month ahead — you both starting new jobs within days of each other.
user: March is going to be hectic.

user: My partner left the design agency and went freelance.
assistant: That was fast — barely two months. What happened?
user: The culture wasn't a fit — very rigid process for creative work. She had three clients lined up already.
assistant: Impressive to have that pipeline ready so quickly.
user: She'd been building it on the side for a while. Calculated risk.

user: Registered for the city half marathon in May. First half-marathon ever.
assistant: Exciting goal — what's your current running base?
user: About 15km per week. I'll need to build to 40km+ by April.
assistant: 16 weeks is manageable with a structured plan. Have you found one?
user: Using Hal Higdon's Novice 1 plan.

user: Rolled my ankle on a trail run. Swollen and tender — took the rest of the week off.
assistant: Ouch. Have you had it looked at?
user: Ice and rest for now. It's not as bad as a sprain — more like a mild twist.
assistant: Any risk to the half-marathon?
user: Race is May 12th. If I can resume running by April 28th I should be okay.

user: Finished the half marathon — 2:07:43. So happy.
assistant: Congratulations! How did it feel out on the course?
user: Miles 8-10 were brutal but I ran the final two kilometers at race pace. Exceeded my 2:15 goal.
assistant: A 7-minute buffer on your target on a first half is excellent.
user: The ankle held up perfectly.

user: Upgraded my phone today — Pixel 8 Pro. Traded in the Pixel 5.
assistant: Nice generational jump. What pushed you to upgrade now?
user: Camera on the Pixel 5 was struggling with low light. The 8 Pro's night mode is in another league.
assistant: Three years on the 5 is a good run.
user: Got $180 trade-in value, which softened the price.

user: Booked the Colombia trip — flights + Airbnb in Medellín for two weeks in October.
assistant: Finally locked in! Departure date?
user: October 5th, back October 19th. Flying into José María Córdova airport.
assistant: Medellín is a fantastic choice. Any non-Medellín stops planned?
user: Day trips to Guatapé and the Coffee Region.

user: Switched from Notion to Obsidian for my personal notes. Been migrating for a week.
assistant: Big switch. What's the main draw?
user: Local-first files, markdown, and the graph view for connecting ideas. Also no monthly fee.
assistant: The plugin ecosystem is rich too.
user: Already installed Dataview and Templater — genuinely powerful.

user: Two weeks into the Obsidian daily note habit. It's sticking.
assistant: What's your daily note format?
user: Morning: priorities + intention. Evening: what happened, what to carry forward.
assistant: That's a solid structure. Using any automation?
user: Templater fills the date header and a mood field automatically.

user: Sold my car today. Listed it two weeks ago on Craigslist — final price $6,800.
assistant: How long did you have it?
user: Six years. A 2016 Honda Civic. Ran well but the bike commute made it feel redundant.
assistant: Decluttering around a lifestyle change — makes sense.
user: And Zipcar is available whenever I actually need a car.

user: Started meditating this week — 10 minutes every morning using the Waking Up app.
assistant: Good morning routine addition. Is this your first structured meditation practice?
user: First one that's lasted more than three days. The Waking Up guided sessions are less intimidating than pure silence.
assistant: Sam Harris's introductory course?
user: Exactly that one.

user: Hit 150 consecutive days of meditation this morning.
assistant: That's a remarkable streak — five months of daily practice.
user: Somewhere around day 40 it stopped feeling like a discipline and started feeling like a necessity.
assistant: What's the most noticeable effect?
user: Reduced reactivity during stressful situations at work. Hard to measure but very real.

user: Got a completely different haircut today — went from shoulder-length to a short crop. Dramatic change.
assistant: Bold move. What prompted it?
user: Wanted something lower-maintenance ahead of the new job. No regrets — feels right.
assistant: First reactions from people around you?
user: Partner loves it. Coworkers at the old job did a double-take.

user: Got my second crop haircut today. Same barber.
assistant: Keeping the shorter style then.
user: Yes, it's become my thing. Took about 15 minutes versus the 45 the long hair needed.
assistant: Efficiency gains.
user: Exactly — morning routine is 10 minutes shorter now.

user: Spring allergies hit hard this year — eyes and nose constantly irritated since last weekend.
assistant: Worst season in a while for pollen apparently.
user: Started taking Zyrtec — it dulls it but doesn't eliminate it.
assistant: Are you mostly affected indoors or outdoors?
user: Outdoors, especially mornings. Bike commute is rough.

user: Allergies have cleared up — feels like spring is over the worst of it.
assistant: Four weeks of misery then a hard stop. Relief!
user: Stopped the Zyrtec three days ago and feeling fine.
assistant: Good timing heading into the warmer months.
user: Perfect timing with the half-marathon training peaking too.

user: First freelance payment came in for my partner — $3,200 for a brand identity project.
assistant: Congratulations to her on the first real freelance invoice paid.
user: She was nervous about cash flow but the pipeline she'd built is paying off.
assistant: Any recurring clients forming yet?
user: Two clients want ongoing retainers — looks promising.

queries.query: When did I sign up for a gym membership?
When did I cancel my gym membership?
How long did I have my gym membership before cancelling?
When did I receive the job offer from Meridian Tech?
How many days passed between receiving the Meridian Tech offer and accepting it?
When did I start at Meridian Tech?
What happened at the dentist before my job started at Meridian?
When did I get my cavity filled?
What did I do on my first day at Meridian Tech?
When did I move to my new apartment?
What changed about my commute after moving?
Which book did I finish before starting at Meridian Tech?
When did I start reading Designing Data-Intensive Applications?
How long did it take me to read DDIA?
What happened to my side project in February?
When did I resume the dotfiles side project?
How long did the dotfiles CLI project take from start to shipping v0.1?
When did I attend a tech conference?
What did I do for learning between starting at Meridian Tech and the QCon conference?
When did I start learning Spanish?
What was my Duolingo streak when I reported on it in April?
When did I switch from Duolingo to in-person Spanish classes?
What changed about my investment allocation in early 2024?
What was my portfolio allocation before the change in March?
When did I adopt my dog?
When did Theo have his first vet checkup?
How long after adopting Theo did I take him to the vet?
When did I ask for a raise at Meridian?
How long did it take between asking for a raise and getting a result?
What was my salary before and after the raise at Meridian?
When did I switch to a pour-over coffee setup?
What coffee equipment upgrade happened more recently — the V60 or the electric grinder?
When did I change my commute podcast?
What podcast was I listening to before I switched to Software Engineering Daily?
When did my partner start her job at the design agency?
How long did my partner stay at the design agency before going freelance?
When did I sign up for the half marathon?
When did I injure my ankle while training?
When did I run the half marathon and what was my finish time?
What was the most recent health issue I mentioned?
When did I upgrade my phone?
When did I book the Colombia vacation?
How long before the trip did I book it, given the trip is in October?
When did I switch from Notion to Obsidian?
When did I sell my car?
Did I sell my car before or after moving to the new apartment?
When did I start meditating?
How many days had I meditated consecutively by the end of June?
When did I get a new haircut style?
What was the most recent significant career event?
